_note: Edit this file to control the research loop. All agents and the orchestration script read this file. Keys prefixed with _ are comments and are ignored by the script.
_workflow_note: DEFAULT WORKFLOW: (1) Claude Code Opus 4.6 executes the study and writes the report. (2) Copilot Codex 5.4 xHigh critically reviews the report as Science Director. (3) If review fails, Codex generates a follow-up prompt sent back to Opus. (4) Loop until Codex approves. (5) Opus performs final readability polish. See AGENTS.md Multi-Agent Research Loop for full protocol.
_model_selection_note: IMPORTANT: Model selection is manual. Select the correct model in the agent picker BEFORE invoking each agent. The model_id values below are the documented intended models — treat them as documentation and as the values to select in the picker. For execution: open Claude Code and select Opus 4.6. For review: open Copilot and select Codex 5.4 xHigh.

models._note: Three roles: execution (primary research agent + report writer), review (science director + critical reviewer), documentation (report writing, same as execution). Select the correct model before invoking @execution-engineer or @science-director.

execution.role: Primary Research Executor and Communicator — full study lifecycle: code writing, simulation, debugging, figure generation, validation, report writing, and documentation
execution._candidates: claude-opus-4-6 (preferred). Falls back to Codex 5.4 xHigh if Opus is unavailable.

review.role: Science Director and Critical Reviewer — research planning, physics reasoning, report quality review, evidence-claim verification, follow-up prompt generation, and APPROVE/REVISE decisions. See science-director.agent.md for the full review protocol.
review._candidates: Codex 5.4 xHigh (required for physics reasoning quality). Do not use a weaker model for review — the reviewer must be capable of rigorous critical assessment.

documentation.role: Report writing — LaTeX generation, figure captions, IMPROVEMENTS.md. Same as execution (Opus is the communicator/writer).
documentation._candidates: claude-opus-4-6 (preferred). Codex 5.4 xHigh acceptable if Opus is unavailable.

loop._note_max_iterations: The reviewer will refuse to issue another REVISE after this many iterations. Set higher if you expect many refinement cycles.

stages.1_execute: Opus 4.6 (Claude Code) runs the study end-to-end (plan → implement → validate → report)
stages.2_review: Codex 5.4 xHigh (Copilot) reviews the completed report as Science Director for technical correctness and writing quality
stages.3_refine: If review fails, Codex generates a FOLLOWUP_PROMPT.md; Opus runs another iteration
stages.4_polish: After technical approval, Opus performs a final readability and coherence pass

stopping_criteria._note: require_all_blockers_resolved=false allows completion with documented open issues. require_review_approval=true means Codex (Science Director) must explicitly issue APPROVE before the study is marked COMPLETE.

retry._note_blocked_policy: continue_with_partial: move to next phase with gaps documented. stop_and_report: halt the loop and write a blocker report.
retry._note_auto_recover: When true, the recover action generates a rich RESUME_PROMPT.md that pre-fills the next Copilot invocation with full context.

report._note_extension_mode: append_iteration_section: adds a new LaTeX \section{} block before \end{document}, never touching prior content. structured_extend: merges into existing structure (requires manual review).
report._note_preserve: If preserve_existing_report=true, the Execution Engineer reads the prior report.tex before writing. New material is always added below the existing content.

review._note: Codex (Science Director) writes REVIEW_DIRECTIVE.md in the task_runs/<study>/ directory after every review. If decision is REVISE or NEEDS_REWORK, it also writes FOLLOWUP_PROMPT.md — a ready-to-paste prompt for the next Opus invocation.

logging._note: recovery_prompt_file is written inside the task_runs/<study>/ directory after every phase transition.
